$schema_note: Test-coverage backlog for the agent-utils repo. Each entry is a test commitment, not a feature spec. See CLAUDE.md for the TDD sequence. runner ∈ {bats, pytest, agent}. requires lists external tools that gate the test (skipped when missing).

entries.description: __gwt_sanitize_name lowercases, replaces / and _ with -, strips leading/trailing -, truncates to 50 chars
__gwt_discover_ports parses ${*_PORT} from .env.template, preserves order, dedupes
Port allocation assigns contiguous block from 40000 using first-fit against .gwt_index
gwt-ports reports the assignment for the current worktree
gwt with no branch arg or no template exits non-zero with a clear message
security-audit --help, version, and setup --check-only succeed without mutation
security-audit writes reports to XDG_DATA_HOME, never inside the scanned repo
scan detects a planted private key via gitleaks (deep profile)
scan flags a known-vulnerable Python dep via trivy
scan flags a SAST code-pattern issue via semgrep
entries.notes: Auto-skips when semgrep is not on PATH; flips true after a green run with semgrep installed.

entries.description: report --latest returns the most recent scan
Every SKILL.md is named exactly SKILL.md and has frontmatter with a lowercase-hyphenated name matching its folder
Every references/*.md is linked from its SKILL.md (no orphans)
README.md skill table includes every top-level skill folder, links to anchors, and has a per-skill section
README mini-help block lists the same top-level commands as bin/<cli> --help
No em-dashes or en-dashes in *.md outside fenced code blocks
SKILL.md descriptions trigger cleanly and don't overlap with siblings
entries.notes: Apply by reading the rubric and walking each SKILL.md frontmatter. Flip true once all criteria green.

entries.description: Each reference doc is scoped to one concern and progressively discloses detail
entries.notes: Apply by walking every references/*.md against the rubric checklist.

entries.description: README per-skill section covers motivation, structure, integrated tools, reference summary
entries.notes: Apply per top-level skill section in README.md.

entries.description: CLAUDE.md still matches actual repo state (paths, conventions, mandates)
entries.notes: Apply on demand, e.g. before a release or after major SKILL/CLI changes.
